windows.embed_text_v2: molten beeswax cooling and setting in three parchment-lined rectangular trays, wax solidifying from translucent orange to opaque yellow, crystallizing surface, honeybees, outdoor table
windows.summary_v2: Three parchment-lined trays of poured molten beeswax cool and solidify outdoors from translucent orange to opaque yellow, with honeybees landing on the set wax.

tags_v2.action: cooling and setting poured beeswax in trays

seconds_v2.desc: Three parchment-lined rectangular trays of molten beeswax sit on a white folding table outdoors; the wax is still hot, translucent orange-amber with a glossy surface. A 'Freckles Viewpoints' watermark sits at lower center.
The cooling wax begins to skin over; a mottled crystallization pattern starts spreading across the two left trays while the right tray stays more liquid-yellow. Watermark still visible.
Crystallization advances; the left and middle slabs show a brain-like wrinkled texture as the surface sets, the wax lightening toward opaque orange-yellow.
The wax continues solidifying from the edges inward, surfaces turning more matte yellow with the mottled pattern covering most of the left two trays.
Most of the wax has gone opaque yellow; only faint swirl marks remain where it was last to set, the right tray nearly fully solid.
The three slabs are now uniform opaque yellow; a honeybee has landed on the right slab's surface.
Cooled yellow beeswax slabs fully set; a couple of honeybees rest on the wax near the tray edges.
Smooth solid yellow beeswax slabs in their parchment-lined trays, surfaces now even and matte.
Several honeybees crawl over the set yellow wax slabs as warm light hits the table; the wax is fully hardened in the moulds.
